title: Phrasal Verbs
topics: daily life · relationships · work · movement · feelings
description: Nejdůležitější frázová slovesa — 45 s příklady v kontextu.
notes: Phrasal verb = verb + particle — meaning often different from the base verb!
⚠️  Some are SEPARABLE: 'turn ON the light' = 'turn the light ON'
⚠️  Some are INSEPARABLE: 'look after HIM' (NOT 'look him after')
✓ With pronoun — always separate: 'pick IT up' (not 'pick up it')

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: I can't find out what time the meeting is. Have you heard?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: No idea. Can you look into it? I need to set off by 4.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: I'll call back after I've dealt with this email.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Fine. And please don't put it off — we need to sort this out today.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Don't worry. I'll come up with a solution.

examples.en: Don't give up — you're nearly there!
She came up with a brilliant idea.
I can't put up with this noise any longer.
We ran out of milk, so I went to the shop.
examples.cz: Došlo nám mléko, takže jsem šel/šla do obchodu.

examples.en: It took months to get over the breakup.
examples.cz: Trvalo měsíce, než se z rozchodu dostal/a.

quiz.sentence: She ___ up very early every morning.
I need to ___ out what time the train leaves.
He ___ up with the idea during the meeting.
Can you ___ after my dog while I'm away?

tasks: 1. Write 10 sentences about your daily routine using phrasal verbs
2. Find 5 phrasal verbs you didn't know before — use each in a sentence
3. Write a short story (10 sentences) using at least 8 phrasal verbs
